representative_input_captured: One source, packet, bibliography entry, or archive item that needs identity, authority, route, and next-action classification.
operation_performed: Classify the item, assign authority status, detect duplicate or supersession risk, route it to a project purpose, and set its claim ceiling.
captured_output_record: A promotion record with identity, provenance, authority decision, route, source boundary, and next build action.

acceptance_results.evidence: One source, packet, bibliography entry, or archive item that needs identity, authority, route, and next-action classification.
Classify the item, assign authority status, detect duplicate or supersession risk, route it to a project purpose, and set its claim ceiling.
The fixture fails if an item can advance without identity, authority, route, and claim-ceiling fields.
This proof fixture is a build instrument. It does not by itself prove final operational standing until executed and captured.
Requires human close reading or source-authenticity review before quotation, citation, or stronger source-use claims.

blocking_items_remaining: The package can capture identity, authority, route, and claim-ceiling logic, but source quotations and external validation still require human review.
claim_boundary_after_capture: May claim a package-level first capture exists. May not claim production deployment, external validation, or full operational standing.
